Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard Hire to Retire Process Expected Post Job Ad then Screen Candidates then Conduct Interviews then Make Hiring Decision then Onboard Employee then Add to Payroll then Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 Structure Interpretation Post happens first Screen happens after Post After Screen both Decide and Interview are enabled No order between Decide and Interview Decide to Onboard to Payroll to Close Interview has no outgoing edges to other activities

Name: Validation - PE
Text: No edge between Interview and Decide So Decide can happen before after or during Interview This is a critical flaw

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Major Anomaly 1 Interview is not ordered before Decide In reality interviews must happen before making a hiring decision Here Decide can happen immediately after Screen completely bypassing or running concurrently with Interview This violates core process logic

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Major Anomaly 2 Interview has no successor It does not lead to Decide Onboard or anything else It is effectively disconnected from the main flow after Screen

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Severity High Fundamentally breaks the hiring logic

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 Structure Interpretation Post enables both Screen and Interview Interview to Decide Decide to Onboard loop to Payroll xor to Close Screen has no outgoing edges

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Screen is only connected from Post It is dangling Interview is connected from Post and to Decide So the critical path is intact

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Major Anomaly 1 Screen is dangling It happens after Post but does not lead to Interview or Decide Screening should logically precede interviews Here interviews can happen directly after posting bypassing screening

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Moderate Minor Anomaly 3 xor payroll allows skipping payroll entirely While in a standard Hire to Retire adding to payroll is mandatory skipping it is a deviation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Moderate Minor Anomaly 4 loop onboarding allows repeating onboarding Onboarding is typically a one time process per hire Repeating it is odd but not fundamentally broken

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 maintains the critical causal link between conducting interviews and making the hiring decision whereas Model 1 allows the hiring decision to be made independently of or concurrently with interviews

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Bypassing screening is a risk but possible Bypassing interviews for a hiring decision is fundamentally broken for standard hiring

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore Model 2 is closer to normative because it preserves Interview to Decide while Model 1 breaks that hard dependency
